data.title: Cullen/Frost Bankers Signals Confident Growth in Earnings
data.description:
Cullen/frost Bankers ((CFR)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cullen/Frost Bankers delivered an upbeat earnings call, underscored by double‑digit profit and EPS growth, healthier margins, and robust loan demand. Management highlighted the strength of its consumer and commercial franchises, while acknowledging pockets of credit and securities‑portfolio pressure that investors should monitor but not yet fear.

Earnings and EPS Growth

Net income rose to $169.3 million, a 13.4% increase from a year earlier, while diluted EPS climbed 15.2% to $2.65. The performance reflects disciplined balance‑sheet management and solid operating leverage, even as some noninterest items introduced quarter‑to‑quarter noise in reported results.

Net Interest Margin Expansion

Net interest margin improved to 3.74% for the quarter, up eight basis points sequentially. This expansion came despite lower average deposits and customer repos, showing the bank’s ability to reprice assets and optimize its funding mix in a still‑challenging rate environment.

Loan and Deposit Growth

Average loans increased to $22.0 billion from $20.8 billion a year ago, or roughly 5.8% growth, reflecting healthy demand across the franchise. Average deposits edged up 1.2% to $42.2 billion year over year, suggesting stable core funding even with some seasonal pressure in the linked‑quarter comparison.

Branch Expansion and EPS Accretion

The branch expansion strategy is proving highly accretive, adding $0.14 to EPS in the quarter, or about 5.6% of earnings. Expansion markets now account for $2.9 billion in loans, $3.6 billion in deposits, and roughly 95,000 new households, with management planning to open another 10–12 locations in 2026.

Consumer Franchise and Mortgage Momentum

Cullen/Frost’s consumer franchise posted strong gains, with consumer checking households growing 5.3% over the past year. Consumer loan balances jumped 19%, including $154 million of growth in the quarter and a $124 million increase in mortgage balances, which reached $719 million and nearly doubled the prior quarter’s pace.

Record Commercial Relationships and Pipeline Strength

Commercial activity accelerated, with 1,016 new relationships in the quarter, the strongest first quarter on record and the fourth straight period above 1,000. The loan pipeline swelled to an all‑time high of $6.8 billion, including about $2.0 billion expected to close in 90 days, up sharply from the prior quarter.

Credit Quality Stable and Improving

Headline credit metrics remain reassuring, with nonperforming assets flat at $73 million and down from $85 million a year ago. Net charge‑offs held at $5.8 million, with annualized losses at 11 basis points of average loans, improved from 19 basis points last year and stable as a share of loans and assets.

Investment Portfolio Yield and Positioning

Management was active in the securities book, purchasing $2.3 billion of investments across Treasuries, agency mortgage‑backed securities, and municipals at attractive stated yields. The overall tax‑equivalent yield on the portfolio ticked up to 3.85%, three basis points higher, supporting margin resilience while rates remain elevated.

Customer Satisfaction as a Strategic Asset

Cullen/Frost again received top consumer‑banking customer‑satisfaction honors in Texas from J.D. Power, marking its 17th consecutive win. The recognition underscores a longstanding service advantage that appears to be translating into organic growth in checking accounts, loans, and new relationships.

Rising Problem Loans and Concentrated Risk

Beneath the benign credit headlines, total problem loans classified at risk grade 10 or higher increased to $989 million from $857 million last quarter. Management emphasized the increase is concentrated in the risk grade 10 category and expressed confidence in sizable resolutions expected over the next two quarters.

Unrealized Losses in the AFS Portfolio

The available‑for‑sale portfolio saw net unrealized losses expand to $1.15 billion from $1.04 billion, a $110 million deterioration. This reflects mark‑to‑market pressure as rates moved, and while it does not hit current earnings, it remains a key factor for tangible book value sensitivity and investor sentiment.

Linked-Quarter Deposit and Repo Headwinds

On a sequential basis, average total deposits fell by $1.1 billion due to seasonal factors, while customer repos declined by $426 million. The funding shifts weighed on net interest income even as they modestly benefited margin, highlighting the trade‑off between volume and spread in the near term.

Expense Volatility and FDIC Charges

Noninterest expenses were choppy, with FDIC assessments rising by $8.6 million following a reversal in the prior quarter. Last quarter also contained one‑time payroll transition costs and elevated stock compensation, making year‑over‑year and sequential comparisons less straightforward for investors tracking efficiency.

Return on Equity Under Modest Pressure

Return on average common equity came in at 15.15%, slightly below 15.54% a year earlier despite better earnings per share. The modest compression suggests that while profitability remains strong, capital levels and business mix shifts are tempering headline returns on equity.

Noninterest Income Seasonality

Other income declined by $4 million from the prior quarter, largely because the bank booked a $5.4 million annual VISA volume bonus in the previous period. This timing issue adds to quarter‑to‑quarter noise in fee revenue, but does not materially change the underlying earnings trajectory.

Guidance and Forward-Looking Outlook

Management’s outlook assumes significant rate cuts in late 2026 and continued branch‑driven growth across loans and deposits from an already sizable expansion base. With a record $6.8 billion loan pipeline, improving net interest margin, tight nonperforming metrics, and manageable problem‑loan exposure, the bank projects sustained momentum even though full net interest income guidance was not completed.

Cullen/Frost’s earnings call painted a picture of a franchise leaning into growth, backed by strong credit performance and a loyal customer base, while carefully managing rate and credit risks. For investors, the near‑term watch items are problem loans and securities marks, but the overall story remains one of solid profitability and expanding opportunity.

data.title: Murray Cod Australia Seeks Trading Halt Ahead of Entitlement Offer Outcome
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Murray Cod Australia Limited ( (AU:MCA) ) is now available.

Murray Cod Australia Limited has requested a trading halt on its securities from the ASX as it undertakes an accelerated non-renounceable entitlement offer aimed at existing shareholders. The halt is designed to ensure trading does not occur on an uninformed basis while the company finalises and communicates the outcome of the institutional component of the offer.

The trading halt will remain in place until before the market opens on 5 May 2026, when Murray Cod Australia is expected to announce the institutional results of the capital raising. The move signals an active step to strengthen the company’s capital position, with implications for its funding flexibility and potential expansion in the aquaculture sector once the entitlement offer is completed.

More about Murray Cod Australia Limited

Murray Cod Australia Limited is an aquaculture company listed on the ASX under the code MCA, focused on breeding and growing Murray cod, a premium freshwater fish species, for domestic and export food markets. The company’s operations centre on large-scale, land-based aquaculture systems in regional Australia, targeting high-value seafood and restaurant channels.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Berkshire Hills Bancorp Earnings Call Balances Progress And Pain
data.description:
Berkshire Hills Bancorp ((BBT)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Berkshire Hills Bancorp’s latest earnings call painted a cautious but constructive picture. Management highlighted strong capital, solid core profitability, and completion of merger integration work, yet admitted to being “very disappointed” with headline earnings as margin pressure, deposit runoff, and rising credit issues cloud the near-term outlook.

Core Systems Conversion Clears the Merger Overhang

Berkshire successfully completed its core systems conversion in mid-February, a key milestone in digesting its recent merger. With major implementation work and merger-related charges largely behind it, management says it can now pivot from integration to execution, targeting revenue growth and deeper efficiency gains.

Capital Strength and Shareholder-Friendly Moves

Regulatory capital remains a clear bright spot, with CET1 at 11% and tangible common equity at 9.1%. Tangible book value inched up to $23.48 per share, and the board supported returns with a quarterly dividend of $0.3225 per share plus authorization for a $50 million share repurchase program, pending regulatory sign-off.

Core Profitability Masks Headline Earnings Weakness

Excluding $13 million of pretax merger charges, operating earnings reached $58.4 million, or $0.70 per share, underscoring healthy underlying profitability. Operating ROA of roughly 1.01% and operating ROTCE above 11% suggest the core bank is generating attractive returns despite a tough rate and funding backdrop.

Expense Discipline Drives Efficiency Gains

Noninterest expenses were essentially flat versus the prior quarter and came in nearly $1 million below internal budget. The operating efficiency ratio, excluding merger charges, landed at about 59.5%, reflecting realized merger synergies and a tight grip on costs as management seeks to offset revenue pressure.

Lower Funding Costs and Strategic Bond Purchases

Interest-bearing deposit costs fell 17 basis points sequentially, a notable reversal after an intense deposit-pricing cycle. The bank also deployed excess liquidity into roughly $130 million of securities with yields around 4.29% and a 3.5 to 3.8-year duration, aiming to support earnings without extending risk too aggressively.

High-Yield, Floating-Rate Loan Production

Loan originations and draws totaled $734 million for the quarter, carrying a weighted average coupon of about 7.63%. Roughly two-thirds of that new volume is floating-rate, positioning the portfolio for attractive yields today while preserving some flexibility as interest rates evolve.

Headline Earnings Miss Amid Margin and Revenue Pressure

GAAP earnings came in at $46.2 million, or $0.55 per share, prompting management to label the results “very disappointed” versus expectations. Net interest income fell to $190.8 million, down 4% quarter-on-quarter, and the net interest margin slipped another four basis points to 3.78%, underscoring ongoing revenue pressure.

Noninterest Income Slumps on Fees and Loan Sales

Fee-based revenue offered little offset, with noninterest income dropping to $23.9 million, down 8% from the prior quarter. The decline was driven by lower deposit fees and weaker gains on loan sales as SBA activity cooled, leaving the bank more exposed to margin-driven earnings swings.

Balance Sheet Shrinks as Deposits Run Off

Total assets fell by about $992 million to $22.2 billion, with loans slipping roughly 1% from the prior quarter. Total deposits declined 6%, though core customer balances were down closer to 2% when excluding payroll and brokered deposits, reflecting seasonal patterns and merger-related runoff rather than broad franchise erosion.

Credit Metrics Weaken With Elevated Charge-Offs

Credit quality showed some strain, as nonperforming loans rose to 83 basis points of total loans. Net charge-offs reached $13.6 million, or 30 basis points annualized, and management signaled that provisioning should run below charge-offs near term as it aggressively works through criticized credits.

Concentrated CRE Stress in Boston and New York

Part of the credit deterioration stems from specific commercial real estate exposures, notably a downtown Boston office loan of about $17 million that moved to nonaccrual with occupancy near 50% and debt service coverage below 1x, though coverage on the exposure is around 40%. The bank’s small New York rent-controlled multifamily book, just seven properties totaling $18 million, also pressured results through a couple of related problem loans.

Lower Purchase Accounting Accretion Dampens Yields

Purchase accounting accretion was about $12.1 million in the quarter, below earlier expectations closer to $15 million. Management now assumes accretion will stay around the $12 million range, trimming a source of elevated yields that had previously padded reported margins and earnings.

Forward Guidance: Soft Near Term, Gradual Improvement

Looking ahead, management expects loan growth to remain soft in the second quarter before strengthening later in the year, while net interest margin is guided to stabilize around 3.80% with gradual improvement. Funding costs should continue to ease, though a parallel 25-basis-point rate cut would shave roughly $6.8 million from annual NII, and credit costs are expected to be manageable as provisions run below charge-offs amid continued workout of criticized loans.

Berkshire Hills Bancorp’s call delivered a blend of structural progress and cyclical pain. Integration is complete, capital is robust, expenses are controlled, and core returns are solid, but weaker margins, shrinking deposits, and rising CRE stress are weighing on reported results. For investors, the story now hinges on management’s ability to convert these structural strengths into resumed growth and cleaner credit through the rest of the year.

data.title: Ava Risk Group Secures Growth Funding as Q3 Orders Lag but Pipeline Remains Strong
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Ava Risk Group Ltd. ( (AU:AVA) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Ava Risk Group reported Q3 FY2026 sales order intake of $6.1 million, taking year-to-date orders to $21.7 million and leaving an order backlog of $6.2 million, including contracted recurring revenue. The company reaffirmed full-year revenue guidance of $34 million to $37 million, while noting order-timing uncertainty in the Middle East due to regional conflict.

The group completed a strategic investment transaction with Hale Capital, securing up to $12.6 million in growth funding via convertible notes and warrants to support U.S. expansion. It advanced key projects across Australian airport perimeter security, government border protection, telecom network protection with Telstra, Middle East energy and border contracts, and U.S. government and corrections sites, and continues its CEO search, expected to conclude in H2 FY2026.

Ava Risk Group Limited is a security technology company focused on advanced fibre optic sensing and intrusion detection systems for critical infrastructure, airports, border protection and telecommunications networks. The group also operates an Access segment providing electronic access control and reader solutions, with a growing emphasis on the U.S. market through distribution partnerships.

data.title: Reedy Lagoon Shares Suspended as ASX Enforces Reporting Deadline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Reedy Lagoon Corporation Limited ( (AU:RLC) ) has shared an update.

Reedy Lagoon Corporation Limited has had its securities suspended from quotation by the ASX after failing to lodge a required periodic report by the specified deadline, alongside several other listed entities. The suspension will remain in place until the outstanding report is submitted, after which trading in the company’s shares is expected to resume on the next trading day, highlighting the compliance pressures on smaller listed issuers and the potential short-term impact on shareholder liquidity.

The action under Listing Rule 17.5 underscores the ASX’s enforcement of timely disclosure requirements, which are central to maintaining market transparency and investor confidence. For Reedy Lagoon shareholders, the suspension temporarily restricts trading but does not in itself indicate any change to the underlying business, with the key near-term focus shifting to the company’s ability to promptly rectify its reporting obligations.

More about Reedy Lagoon Corporation Limited

Reedy Lagoon Corporation Limited is an ASX-listed company operating in the resources sector, with its securities traded under the ticker RLC on the Australian Securities Exchange. The company is part of a group of resource-focused and related entities subject to ASX continuous disclosure and periodic reporting obligations for listed securities.

data.title: NFT Limited Delays Annual 20-F Filing
data.description:
NFT ( (MI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NFT Limited has notified regulators that it will delay filing its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company submitted a Form 12b-25 to explain the late annual report, which is a standard way for foreign issuers to request a short extension.

The company says it needs more time to complete its review of the financial statements before finalizing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report). It does not cite any specific external issues such as auditor changes, internal control failures, or special committee investigations.

NFT Limited expects to file the delayed Form 20-F (Yearly Report) no later than the fifteenth calendar day after the original due date. This is the maximum grace period allowed under the 12b-25 rules for an annual filing.

The company states that it does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year. Investors should still treat any expectations about timing and future results as forward-looking statements that may differ from the final audited numbers.

NFT Limited affirms that all of its other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Yanying Wang on April 30, 2026, signaling management’s commitment to completing the annual report and maintaining compliance.

More about NFT

data.description:
MEDIROM Healthcare Technologies ( (MRM) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Medirom Healthcare Technologies Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The Tokyo-based wellness company, listed in the U.S., is pushing back its annual SEC filing for that period.

The company says it cannot file the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) on time because the audit of its consolidated financial statements for 2025 is still in progress. Management notes that completing the audit and the related internal review would require unreasonable effort and expense within the original deadline.

Medirom has not committed to the typical 15-day extension window under the applicable SEC rule, leaving the exact filing date open. Investors should assume the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) will be submitted once the audit work and management review are finalized, but without a precise timetable.

Importantly for shareholders, the company does not expect any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year. There is no indication in the filing of major restatements, unusual losses, or large swings in earnings, although all statements remain subject to completion of the audit and carry the usual forward-looking uncertainty.

Medirom states that it has been current with all other required SEC reports over the past 12 months, signaling ongoing attention to compliance despite this delay. The notification is signed by Chief Financial Officer Fumitoshi Fujiwara, underscoring executive-level responsibility for completing the audit and bringing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) filing back on schedule.

data.title: RedCloud Holdings Delays Annual 20-F (Yearly Report)
data.description:
RedCloud Holdings plc ( (RCT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

RedCloud Holdings plc has filed a Form 12b-25, announcing a delay in submitting its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The company is seeking brief relief from the U.S. disclosure timetable while it finalizes its audited accounts.

The primary reason for the delay is a change in RedCloud’s independent public accountant, which has made it impractical to complete the audit on time without unreasonable effort or expense. This is an internal process issue rather than a regulatory or enforcement problem.

RedCloud says it expects to file the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) within the standard fifteen-day grace period allowed under SEC rules. Investors should therefore anticipate the full annual filing in the near term, assuming no further complications arise.

Preliminary figures point to revenue of about $48.5 million for 2025, up 4.4% from $46.5 million in 2024, signaling modest top-line growth. The company expects an operating loss of roughly $43.6 million and a net loss of about $46.2 million, showing a wider operating loss but an improved bottom line versus the prior year’s $50.7 million net loss.

Management cautions that these numbers are estimates and may change once the audit is completed and the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) is filed, so they qualify as forward-looking and subject to revision. RedCloud confirms that all other required SEC reports over the past 12 months have been filed, underscoring ongoing compliance.

The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer and Director Justin Floyd, dated April 30, 2026. This signature affirms management’s responsibility for the disclosure and signals continued engagement with regulators and investors as the year-end accounts are finalized.

The most recent analyst rating on (RCT) stock is a Buy with a $5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on RedCloud Holdings plc stock, see the RCT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RCT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RCT is a Neutral.

The score is primarily weighed down by weak financial health: heavy losses, negative cash flow, and a balance sheet showing negative equity despite strong revenue growth.
